tests.title: initCameraMotion and updateCamera are exported functions
tests.description: The cameraMotion module exports initCameraMotion(seed) and updateCamera(camera, elapsedMs, bassEnergy, motionAmplitude) as callable functions.
steps: Import initCameraMotion and updateCamera from src/visual/cameraMotion
Assert both are functions

tests.title: updateCamera changes camera position over time
tests.description: Calling updateCamera at different elapsed times produces different camera.position values, confirming the camera moves autonomously.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera with position and lookAt
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Record camera position after updateCamera at elapsedMs=0
Record camera position after updateCamera at elapsedMs=5000
Record camera position after updateCamera at elapsedMs=30000
Assert at least two of the three positions differ from each other

tests.title: Camera movement spans all three axes for parallax
tests.description: Over a 60-second sweep, the camera position changes on x, y, and z axes independently, revealing depth parallax.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Sample camera positions at 1-second intervals from 0 to 60000ms
Compute the range (max - min) for each axis
Assert x range > 0.05, y range > 0.05, z range > 0.05
acceptance_criteria: Camera movement reveals parallax across depth

tests.title: Movement is smooth — consecutive frame deltas are small
tests.description: At ~60fps (16ms steps), frame-to-frame camera position deltas are small, ensuring no jumps or discontinuities.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Step through 10000ms to 12000ms in 16ms increments
For each step, record camera.position after updateCamera
Assert the Euclidean distance between consecutive positions is < 0.05

tests.title: Movement is deterministic — same seed produces identical positions
tests.description: initCameraMotion with the same seed and identical elapsed times produces bit-identical camera positions, confirming seeded determinism.
steps: Create two mock PerspectiveCameras
Call initCameraMotion('det-seed') and updateCamera on camera A at t=45000
Clear cache if exposed, call initCameraMotion('det-seed') and updateCamera on camera B at t=45000
Assert camera A position equals camera B position exactly

tests.title: Different seeds produce different camera paths
tests.description: Two different seeds yield different camera positions at the same elapsed time, confirming per-session uniqueness.
steps: Create two mock PerspectiveCameras
Call initCameraMotion('seed-alpha') and updateCamera on camera A at t=30000
Call initCameraMotion('seed-beta') and updateCamera on camera B at t=30000
Assert camera A and camera B positions differ on at least one axis

tests.title: Pointer input does not affect camera position
tests.description: updateCamera does not accept or use pointer coordinates. Camera position is identical regardless of any pointer state in the system.
steps: Verify updateCamera function signature has no pointer parameter
Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion and updateCamera at t=20000 with bassEnergy=0.5 and motionAmplitude=1.0
Record the resulting camera position
Call updateCamera again at the same t, bassEnergy, and motionAmplitude
Assert positions are identical (no external state leakage from pointer)
acceptance_criteria: User pointer does not directly control orbit

tests.title: Bass energy modulates camera orbit radius
tests.description: Higher bassEnergy values produce larger position offsets from the base camera position, creating a push/pull parallax effect on bass hits.
steps: Create two mock PerspectiveCameras
Call initCameraMotion with the same seed
Call updateCamera on camera A at t=30000 with bassEnergy=0.0, motionAmplitude=1.0
Call updateCamera on camera B at t=30000 with bassEnergy=1.0, motionAmplitude=1.0
Compute distance from base position (0,0,5) for each camera
Assert camera B distance >= camera A distance (bass widens orbit)

tests.title: motionAmplitude=0.2 produces smaller offsets than motionAmplitude=1.0
tests.description: Reduced motion preference (motionAmplitude=0.2) scales all camera displacement, resulting in subtler movement for accessibility.
steps: Create two mock PerspectiveCameras
Call initCameraMotion with the same seed
Call updateCamera on camera A at t=30000 with bassEnergy=0.5, motionAmplitude=0.2
Call updateCamera on camera B at t=30000 with bassEnergy=0.5, motionAmplitude=1.0
Compute Euclidean distance from base (0,0,5) for each
Assert camera A offset is less than or equal to camera B offset
Assert camera A offset is approximately 0.2x of camera B offset (within tolerance)

tests.title: Camera stays within safe bounds — never drifts too far from origin
tests.description: Over a long sweep (0-300s), camera position stays within reasonable bounds so geometry remains in view.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Sample positions every second from 0 to 300000ms
For each sample, assert |x| < 3, |y| < 3, z is between 2 and 8
This ensures geometry near origin is always visible
acceptance_criteria: Camera movement reveals parallax across depth

tests.title: Camera lookAt target stays near origin
tests.description: The camera always looks toward a point near the origin so the point cloud subject remains centered in view.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera that records lookAt calls
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Call updateCamera at several elapsed times (0, 15000, 30000, 60000)
For each call, verify lookAt was called with coordinates where |x| < 1, |y| < 1, |z| < 1
acceptance_criteria: Camera movement reveals parallax across depth

tests.title: No hard resets — scanning 0 to 120s the max frame-to-frame delta is bounded
tests.description: Sweeping through 120 seconds at 16ms steps, no single frame jump exceeds a safe threshold, confirming no discontinuities.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Step from 0 to 120000ms in 16ms increments
Track max Euclidean distance between consecutive frame positions
Assert max delta < 0.1 per frame

tests.title: Harmonic cache is reused for same seed
tests.description: Calling initCameraMotion with the same seed multiple times reuses cached harmonics, producing identical results without recomputing PRNG sequences.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion('cache-seed')
Call updateCamera at t=50000 and record position
Call initCameraMotion('cache-seed') again (should hit cache)
Call updateCamera at t=50000 and record position
Assert both positions are identical

tests.title: No forbidden storage APIs accessed during camera motion
tests.description: Neither initCameraMotion nor updateCamera access localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies, per privacy requirements.
steps: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem, Storage.prototype.setItem, and document.cookie setter
Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion('privacy-seed')
Call updateCamera at several time values
Assert none of the storage spies were called

tests.title: Organic non-repeating motion within 5 minutes
tests.description: Camera x-position sampled every second for 5 minutes shows no identical 30-sample subsequences, confirming incommensurate harmonic frequencies prevent visible repetition.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Sample camera.position.x every 1000ms from 0 to 300000ms (300 samples)
Round each to 4 decimal places
Check for any repeated 30-sample subsequence
Assert no repeats found

tests.title: renderLoop integrates camera motion — updateCamera called each frame
tests.description: When the render loop is running with a seed, updateCamera is invoked each frame after evolveParams and before renderer.render, applying autonomous camera motion.
steps: Mock the cameraMotion module
Set up a minimal render loop with a seed and geometry system
Trigger several animation frames
Assert initCameraMotion was called once with the session seed
Assert updateCamera was called each frame with the camera, elapsed time, bass energy, and motionAmplitude

tests.title: motionAmplitude=0 results in no camera displacement
tests.description: When motionAmplitude is 0, camera position should remain at the base position with no displacement, as all offsets are scaled to zero.
steps: Create a mock PerspectiveCamera at base position (0,0,5)
Call initCameraMotion with a test seed
Call updateCamera at t=30000 with bassEnergy=0.5, motionAmplitude=0
Assert camera position is at or very near (0,0,5) — within epsilon 0.001
